title: Restore the Midfield Triangle
description: You are a central midfielder. The team is building out of the middle third. Your triangle has broken down — reposition to restore the passing structure and relieve pressure on the ball carrier.
teaching_point: Step off the opponent's shoulder into the triangle gap to give the ball carrier a safe passing option and restore the midfield shape.

feedback_hints.success: You found the gap in the triangle and gave the ball carrier a clean pass option.
feedback_hints.common_error: Staying in the opponent's shadow means the ball carrier has no safe option and may be forced backward.
feedback_hints.alternate_valid: A slightly deeper position still breaks the press — the ball carrier has an outlet.
feedback_hints.teaching_emphasis: The triangle only works when all three corners are visible to each other. Step off the shoulder to create the angle.
success_points: You stepped off the opponent's shoulder into the triangle gap, creating a clear passing angle.
Your position gave the ball carrier a safe outlet and helped restore the midfield shape.
The triangle re-formed around your run, allowing the ball to progress through pressure.
error_points: You stayed in the opponent's shadow, blocking the passing lane and keeping the triangle broken.
Your position was too close to the presser — the ball carrier could not play safely through the gap.
The triangle could not form without you stepping into the gap between opponents.
alternate_points: A slightly deeper position still offered the ball carrier a usable outlet even outside the ideal pocket.
Your run still created an option even if it did not fully restore the triangle shape.

on_success.explanation: The midfield triangle is restored — the ball carrier has a safe pass into the pocket and can play out of pressure.

on_failure.explanation: The triangle cannot form — the ball carrier is forced sideways or backward under continued pressure.
